engagement: PR-482 authorized adversary emulation (deduped finding set only)
rules_of_engagement: Staging mirror of acme/payments-gateway @ f9e8d7c; PoC validation on triage.blocking_candidates only (T-001, T-002, T-003); no production traffic.
threat_model: Internet-facing payment webhook; techniques from threat_intel.json (T1190, T1212, T1552.001).

attack_chain.name: Unsecured Credentials: Credentials In Files
attack_chain.action: Recover the committed webhook signing secret (whsec_5f3b9c2a...) from the PR's config commit c4d5e6f.

attack_chain.name: Exploitation for Credential Access
attack_chain.action: Author a malicious Stripe event and compute a valid HMAC-SHA256 with the recovered secret; the non-constant-time compare (:96) would also leak the signature byte-by-byte even without the secret.
attack_chain.result: valid Stripe-Signature forged; webhook accepts attacker-authored event as genuine

attack_chain.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_chain.action: Inside the now-trusted event, set Stripe-Event-Id to a SQLi payload; it reaches the idempotency query (:147) and the audit insert (:201) unparameterized.
attack_chain.result: read/write to idempotency + webhook_audit tables; demonstrated extraction of event-ledger rows (counts only, no real data exfiltrated per RoE)

validated.note: Forged a valid signature end-to-end on staging. This is the keystone: it converts the SQLi from 'requires a valid Stripe call' to 'remotely reachable by any attacker'.
Injection reproduced through the forged-signature path; both sinks (the merged root cause) reachable with one payload.

NOT reproducible: although the vulnerable golang-jwt v3.2.0 verifier is linked, the call site already pins HS256 via an explicit method check before Parse, so the algorithm-confusion PoC did not chain here. Still upgrade (latent + brittle), but it is not a merge blocker on this PR. This is exactly why red-team runs after triage: CVSS said High+reachable, emulation said not-exploitable-as-written.

objective_outcome: OBJECTIVE ACHIEVED — attacker can forge webhook authenticity (T-002) and pivot to SQL injection (T-001), reaching the event ledger from the public internet with no credentials.
